_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-06-29, Boston Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Routes: Germany won Group E (7-1 Curacao, 2-1 Ivory Coast, 1-2 Ecuador; 6 pts, rating 88) -- strong attack but the 7-1 vs minnow Curacao inflates the goal record, and they LOST the dead rubber to Ecuador, so not flawless. Paraguay qualified as a BEST THIRD from Group D (1-4 USA, 1-0 Turkey, 0-0 Australia; 4 pts, GD -2, GF 2, rating 70) -- a disciplined, defensive, low-scoring side (two clean-ish games, only broke down vs USA). So this is the favourite-vs-organized-low-block archetype (England-Ghana / Canada-South Africa, but a bigger class gap): Germany the heavy favourite, but Paraguay sits deep and grinds, keeping it tight -> do NOT expect a rout despite the rating gap, and the tight game RAISES the ET/penalty share, lifting Paraguay's advancement above its 90' win. Full motivation, no rotation. Home = Germany (nominal; neutral venue). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Germany the heavy favourite by class; Paraguay a disciplined low block (0-0 Australia, 1-0 Turkey) that qualified as a best third -> expect a tight, low-scoring game, not a rout, with a real chance Paraguay drags it to extra time.

home.name: Germany
notes: Quality (rating 88); strong attack but the 7-1 vs Curacao inflates it and they lost 1-2 to Ecuador. attack 1.30.
Decent but leaked to Ecuador. defense 0.92.
Heavy favourite, but faces a packed block -> not a rout; tight game read (rho -0.08).

away.name: Paraguay
notes: Disciplined, low-scoring (0-0 Australia, 1-0 Turkey, GF 2 in the group). attack 0.78 -- limited going forward, but no over-downgrade.
Organized block, hard to break down. defense 0.95 -> keeps it tight.
Knockout: a tight game drags toward ET/penalties, lifting the underdog's advancement above its 90' win.

_postmatch.result: Germany 1-1 Paraguay, Paraguay win on penalties -> PARAGUAY ADVANCE. The big upset of the round: the 74.4% favourite goes out.
_postmatch.model_1x2: Germany 58.9 / Draw 25.3 / Paraguay 15.8 (MEDIUM, 90'); advancement Germany 74.4 / Paraguay 25.6
_postmatch.verdict: Mark 🟡: the advancement SIDE missed (the favourite went out), but the GAME played out almost exactly as framed, and the upset arrived through the precise mechanism the model priced. The forecast was the favourite-vs-organized-block archetype: a tight, low-scoring game (total xG 2.50, Under 2.5 54%) that Paraguay's disciplined block drags toward extra time/penalties. It finished 1-1 (a top-3 scoreline, 11.9%; Under 2.5), went to a shootout (the model's ~13% penalties branch), and Paraguay won the 50/50 lottery. This is the clearest vindication of the knockout/advancement framing AS A CONCEPT: I explicitly set Paraguay's advancement (25.6%) ABOVE its 90' win (15.8%) precisely because a tight game gives the underdog penalty equity -- and that equity cashed. It is NOT a process error or a speculative misjudgement (unlike South Korea-South Africa): a correctly-priced 74% favourite still goes out ~1 in 4, and this was one, decided by the irreducible coin-flip of a shootout. The only texture miss was BTTS (leaned No 54.7%, came in Yes -- both scored once).
_postmatch.lesson: An upset of a well-calibrated favourite is not a model failure -- it is the priced tail landing. The advancement framing earned its keep here even though the favourite lost: it correctly located Paraguay's real chance in the PENALTY LOTTERY (a tight game) rather than in winning in 90'. Report advancement, respect the block, and accept that ~25% underdogs win shootouts. Penalties are a coin-flip the model treats as 50/50, and that is honest -- this one landed for the underdog.
